cases.focus: default request emits all link categories, OPML header action output, escaped titles, link URLs, and updated timestamps
numeric link_cat request narrows get_categories include and returns only that category
encoded link_cat is urldecoded and absint-normalized before include selection
the all token is preserved through include selection and treated as all categories by the fixture data provider
the zero token is empty under PHP rules and falls back to the default all-category query

side_effect_policy.timestamp_normalization: dateCreated is normalized to <normalized-date> while preserving OPML shape

opml:default-all-categories.body:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<opml version="1.0">
	<head>
		<title>
		Links for Fixture & Links <Site>		</title>
		<dateCreated><normalized-date> GMT</dateCreated>
				<ownerName>Fixture OPML Head</ownerName>
	</head>
	<body>
<outline type="category" title="Filtered News & Updates">
	<outline text="Filtered Alpha & Omega" type="link" xmlUrl="https://feeds.example.test/alpha.xml" htmlUrl="https://example.test/alpha?x=1&y=2" updated="
							2026-06-01 02:03:04" />
		<outline text="Filtered Unsafe URL" type="link" xmlUrl="" htmlUrl="" updated="
							" />
		</outline>
	<outline type="category" title="Filtered Dev <Links>">
	<outline text="Filtered Haxe <Compiler>" type="link" xmlUrl="https://feeds.example.test/haxe.xml" htmlUrl="https://haxe.example.test/" updated="
							2026-06-02 03:04:05" />
		</outline>
	<outline type="category" title="Filtered Empty Category">
	</outline>
	</body>
</opml>

remaining_gaps.detail: The fixture executes only wp-links-opml.php with deterministic link stubs. RSS/Atom feed templates, post embed rendering, REST oEmbed dispatch, and browser-observed installed routing remain later gates.
The fixture stubs get_categories and get_bookmarks. Database-backed bookmark storage, admin link-manager workflows, and legacy ecosystem behavior remain later coverage.
The fixture compares copied oracle PHP in both roots; generated original-path PHP replacement remains a later cross-domain gate.
